title: Semi join scans all partitions instead of pruning
description:
### What happens?

Pruning happens on the left side but not on the right side.
 
Workaround: use LEFT JOIN pattern (longer) instead as file filter extends to right side.

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE orders1 AS 
  SELECT 1 id, 'A' cust, 2023 yr, 1 mth UNION 
  SELECT 2, 'B', 2023, 2 UNION
  SELECT 3, 'C', 2024, 1;

CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE orders2 AS 
  SELECT 1 id, 'A' cust, 2023 yr, 1 mth UNION 
  SELECT 2, 'B', 2023, 2 UNION
  SELECT 4, 'C', 2024, 4;

COPY orders1 TO 'orders1' (FORMAT PARQUET, PARTITION_BY (yr, mth), overwrite 1);
COPY orders2 TO 'orders2' (FORMAT PARQUET, PARTITION_BY (yr, mth), overwrite 1);


explain analyze select * FROM 'orders1/**/*.parquet'
  SEMI JOIN 'orders2/**/*.parquet' USING(yr, mth)
  where yr=2024;
```

Outcome: all (3 here) files of `orders2` are scanned
Expected: only one, as year=2024 file filter should pass on (like other joins)

`ANTI JOIN` has probably the same problem


### OS:

Win amd64

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.2

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE orders3 (id INTEGER, cust VARCHAR(255), yr INTEGER, mth INTEGER, amount DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE TABLE orders4 (id INTEGER, cust VARCHAR(255), yr INTEGER, mth INTEGER, status BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO orders4 VALUES (1, 'D', 2023, 1, TRUE), (2, 'E', 2023, 2, FALSE), (3, 'F', 2024, 1, NULL);
SELECT * FROM orders3 a LEFT JOIN orders4 b ON a.yr = b.yr AND a.mth = b.mth WHERE a.yr = 2024;
key_bug_features: SEMI JOIN does not prune partitions correctly
